name: Deloitte — 2026 Healthcare Outlook
description: 85% of leaders plan to increase agentic AI investment, 59% expect 20%+ cost savings
statistic: 85% of healthcare leaders plan to increase investment in agentic AI over the next 2-3 years, with 61% already building or implementing agentic AI initiatives or having secured budgets. Among 'early adopters,' 59% expect cost savings exceeding 20% within 2-3 years, compared to only 13% of 'watchers.' 98% of all surveyed executives expect at least 10% cost savings. 82% of early adopters prioritise multi-agent solutions across operations. An 'AI divide' is emerging between large organisations rapidly scaling agentic AI and smaller organisations moving cautiously. Adoption barriers are easing: 40% say technical talent is no longer a major challenge, 38% report reduced friction from resistance to change, and 35% cite improved leadership buy-in.
date: February 11, 2026 (agentic AI report); December 11, 2025 (2026 US Health Care Outlook). Survey conducted September 2025.
credibility: Consultancy — Deloitte is a Big Four professional services firm. Survey of 100 healthcare technology executives (50 health systems, 50 health plans) plus 35 focus group participants. Separate outlook survey of 120 C-suite executives from organisations with revenues exceeding $500 million.
applicability: Healthcare adjacent — US-focused survey of health systems and health plans, but findings on agentic AI adoption, cost savings expectations, and the 'AI divide' are directly transferable to UK care sector context where similar adoption gaps exist between early movers and laggards.
citable: Yes — published on Deloitte Insights with clear methodology. Attribute to 'Deloitte, Many health care leaders are leaning into agentic AI, February 2026.' Note: US healthcare context, so cite with that caveat for UK audience.
website_placement: Social proof bar or stat block — '85% of healthcare leaders are increasing AI investment' is a powerful social proof stat. The '59% expect 20%+ cost savings' works well in an ROI or business case section. The 'AI divide' framing is ideal for a fear-of-missing-out section targeting care providers who are hesitant about AI adoption.
emotional_vs_rational: Primarily rational — hard percentages on investment intent and cost savings appeal to CEO/owner and operations director decision-making. Secondary emotional trigger through the 'AI divide' framing, which creates urgency and fear of being left behind as a 'watcher' while competitors gain advantages as 'early adopters.'
clarica_differentiator: Strong differentiator. The emergence of 'agentic AI' as a distinct category beyond basic generative AI validates Clarica's positioning as an AI agency (not just a software vendor). The finding that early adopters favour multi-agent solutions (82%) supports Clarica's consultative, bespoke approach over one-size-fits-all software. The 'AI divide' narrative helps Clarica frame its services as the bridge that prevents care providers from falling into the 'watcher' category.
refresh_frequency: Annual — Deloitte publishes healthcare outlook reports each December/January. The agentic AI-specific report is newer (February 2026) and may be refreshed annually as the category matures. Next update expected December 2026 / early 2027.
comparison_pair: Early adopters: 59% expect 20%+ cost savings from agentic AI. Watchers: only 13% expect similar savings. This 46-percentage-point gap quantifies the cost of inaction. Also: only one-third of organisations operate AI at scale vs 49% still experimenting — showing most of the sector has yet to realise the benefits.
